content.content:
1	# Architecture
2	
3	**Parent:** [`spec.md`](spec.md) · **Siblings:** [`skills-protocol.md`](skills-protocol.md) · [`agent-adapters.md`](agent-adapters.md) · [`modes.md`](modes.md)
4	
5	This doc describes the system topology, runtime modes, data flow, and file layout. Design rationale lives in [`spec.md`](spec.md); protocol details for skills and agent adapters live in their own docs.
6	
7	[ocod]: https://github.com/OpenCoworkAI/open-codesign
8	[acd]: https://github.com/VoltAgent/awesome-claude-design
9	[piai]: https://github.com/badlogic/pi-mono/tree/main/packages/ai
10	[guizang]: https://github.com/op7418/guizang-ppt-skill
11	
12	---
13	
14	## 1. Three deployment topologies
15	
16	OD is a web app plus a local daemon. The split means the same UI can run in three shapes:
17	
18	### Topology A — Fully local (the default)
19	
20	```
21	┌────────────────── user's machine ──────────────────┐
22	│                                                    │
23	│   browser ──► Next.js dev server (localhost:3000)  │
24	│                       │                            │
25	│                       │ http://localhost:7456      │
26	│                       ▼                            │
27	│            od daemon (Node, long-running)         │
28	│                       │                            │
29	│                       ▼                            │
30	│            spawns: claude / codex / cursor / …     │
31	└────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘
32	```
33	
34	One `pnpm tools-dev run web` starts both the Next.js app and the daemon. `pnpm tools-dev` adds the desktop shell. Zero config. No accounts.
35	
36	### Topology B — Web on Vercel + daemon on user's machine
37	
38	```
39	browser ──► od.yourdomain.com (Vercel)
40	              │
41	              │ ws(s):// user-provided URL (e.g. cloudflared tunnel)
42	              ▼
43	        od daemon on user's laptop
44	              │
45	              ▼
46	        spawns: claude / codex / …
47	```
48	
49	The user runs `od daemon --expose` which prints a tunnel URL; they paste the URL into the deployed web app's "Connect daemon" screen. Daemon holds secrets; Vercel holds nothing sensitive.
50	
51	### Topology C — Web on Vercel + direct API (no daemon)
52	
53	```
54	browser ──► od.yourdomain.com (Vercel serverless)
55	                       │
56	                       ▼
57	              Anthropic Messages API (BYOK stored in browser)
58	```
59	
60	No local CLI, no daemon. Degraded experience — no Claude Code skills, no filesystem artifacts (stored in IndexedDB), no PPTX export. But it's the "just try it" path. Keys stored `localStorage` with explicit warning.
61	
62	The three topologies share the same web bundle; the difference is which transports are enabled.
63	
64	## 2. Component diagram (logical)
65	
66	```
67	┌─────────────────────────────── Web App ─────────────────────────────┐
68	│                                                                     │
69	│  ┌──────────┐  ┌─────────────┐  ┌───────────┐  ┌────────────────┐  │
70	│  │ chat pane│  │ artifact    │  │ preview   │  │ comment /      │  │
71	│  │          │  │ tree        │  │ iframe    │  │ slider overlay │  │
72	│  └────┬─────┘  └──────┬──────┘  └─────┬─────┘  └────────┬───────┘  │
73	│       │               │               │                  │           │
74	│       └─────────── session bus (in-memory) ──────────────┘           │
75	│                        │                                             │
76	│                        ▼                                             │
77	│              Transport layer (daemon SSE | api-direct | browser)      │
78	└─────────────────────────┬───────────────────────────────────────────┘
79	                          │
80	  ┌───────────────────────┴────────────────────────────────┐
81	  │                                                        │
82	  ▼ (topology A/B)                                         ▼ (topology C)
83	┌─────────────────────── Daemon ───────────────────────┐  ┌────────────┐
84	│                                                      │  │ browser-   │
85	│  session manager      skill registry                 │  │ only       │
86	│  agent adapter pool   design-system resolver         │  │ runtime    │
87	│  artifact store       preview compile pipeline       │  │ (limited)  │
88	│  export pipeline      detection service              │  └────────────┘
89	│                                                      │
90	└─┬────────────────────────────────────────────────┬───┘
91	  │                                                │
92	  ▼                                                ▼
93	┌─ agent CLIs ─┐                           ┌─ filesystem ─┐
94	│ claude       │                           │ ./.od/      │
95	│ codex        │                           │ ~/.od/      │
96	│ cursor-agent │                           │ skills/      │
97	│ gemini       │                           │ DESIGN.md    │
98	│ opencode     │                           └──────────────┘
99	│ qwen         │
100	└──────────────┘
101	```
102	
103	## 3. Key components
104	
105	### 3.1 Web app (Next.js 16, App Router)
106	
107	- **Why Next.js, not Vite SPA?** We want SSR for the marketing landing page + serverless routes for Topology C's direct-API path + Vercel deployment as a first-class citizen. An SPA would need a separate server for any of that.
108	- **State:** React/browser state for UI config, with projects/conversations/files hydrated from the daemon APIs.
109	- **Iframe preview:** Vendored React 18 + Babel standalone for JSX artifacts, following [Open CoDesign][ocod]'s approach. HTML artifacts load raw. See [§5](#5-preview-renderer).
110	- **Comment mode:** Click captures `[data-od-id]` on preview DOM, opens a popover, sends `{artifact_id, element_id, note}` to daemon → agent gets a surgical edit instruction.
111	- **Slider UI:** When an agent emits a "tweak parameter" tool call (see [`skills-protocol.md`](skills-protocol.md) §4.2), the web app renders a live-update control that re-sends parameterized prompts without round-tripping the chat.
112	
113	### 3.2 Local daemon (`od daemon`)
114	
115	Single binary via `pkg` or a thin Node script distributed over npm. Responsibilities:
116	
117	- Listen on `http://localhost:7456` by default. Accept REST/SSE routes under `/api/*`.
118	- Maintain a **session** per web tab. Sessions hold: active agent, active skill, active artifact, in-flight tool calls, design-system reference.
119	- Operate the **agent adapter pool**: one detected CLI = one adapter instance, reused across sessions.
120	- Scan and index **skills** from `~/.claude/skills/`, `./skills/`, `./.claude/skills/` on startup and on FS-watch events.
121	- Own the **artifact store** — writes files to disk, never in memory.
122	- Run the **preview compile pipeline** (Babel transform for JSX, CSS inliner for HTML exports).
123	- Provide export hooks for HTML/PDF/ZIP and skill-defined deck outputs.
124	
125	### 3.3 Agent adapter pool
126	
127	See [`agent-adapters.md`](agent-adapters.md) for the full interface. Each adapter:
128	
129	1. **Detects** its target CLI (PATH lookup + config-dir probe).
130	2. **Spawns** the CLI with a standardized wrapper prompt + skill context + design-system context + CWD set to the project's artifact root.
131	3. **Streams** stdout/stderr as structured events (JSON Lines if the CLI supports it; line-based parser otherwise).
132	4. **Reports capabilities** — does it support multi-turn? Surgical edits? Native skill loading? Tool use?
133	
134	### 3.4 Skill registry
135	
136	See [`skills-protocol.md`](skills-protocol.md). Scans three locations and merges:
137	
138	| Source | Priority | Purpose |
139	|---|---|---|
140	| `./.claude/skills/` | highest | project-private skills |
141	| `./skills/` | medium | project-declared skills |
142	| `~/.claude/skills/` | lowest | user-global skills |
143	
144	Conflicts resolve by priority (higher wins). Each skill parsed once; watched for changes in dev.
145	
146	### 3.5 Design-system resolver
147	
148	- Looks for `./DESIGN.md` first, then `./design-system/DESIGN.md`, then user-configured path.
149	- Parses the 9-section format (see [awesome-claude-design][acd] schema).
150	- Injects as a prepended system message on every agent run, plus as a `{{ design_system }}` template variable skills can reference.
151	- Hot-reloads on file change in dev.
152	
153	### 3.6 Artifact store
154	
155	Plain files on disk. Conventional layout per project:
156	
157	```
158	./.od/
159	├── config.json                  # project-level daemon config
160	├── artifacts/
161	│   ├── 2026-04-24T10-03-12-landing/
162	│   │   ├── artifact.json        # metadata (skill, mode, prompt, parent)
163	│   │   ├── index.html           # primary output (or .jsx, .md, .pptx.json)
164	│   │   └── assets/              # skill-generated images, fonts, etc.
165	│   └── …
166	├── history.jsonl                # append-only action log (generations, edits, comments)
167	└── sessions/
168	    └── <session-id>.json        # transient; garbage-collected after 24h
169	```
170	
171	Rationale:
172	- **Plain files** → users can `git add ./.od/artifacts/` and review designs in PRs.
173	- **`artifact.json` metadata** → OD can reconstruct the artifact tree without a DB.
174	- **`history.jsonl` not SQLite** → append-only, git-friendly, greppable. [Open CoDesign][ocod] uses SQLite; we deliberately don't.
175	- **Sessions separate from artifacts** → sessions are ephemeral UI state; artifacts are durable.
176	
177	### 3.7 Export pipeline
178	
179	| Format | How |
180	|---|---|
181	| HTML (self-contained) | Inline all CSS, rewrite asset URLs to data: URIs |
182	| PDF | `puppeteer` → `page.pdf()` on the rendered HTML |
183	| PPTX | `deck-skill` outputs a JSON intermediate (`slides.json`); `pptxgenjs` generates the `.pptx` |
184	| ZIP | `archiver` over `artifacts/<id>/` |
185	| Markdown | direct copy if artifact is `.md`, otherwise skill-defined render |
186	
187	## 4. Data flow — a typical "generate prototype" turn
188	
189	```
190	1. User types prompt in web chat.
191	2. Web sends { method: "session.generate", params: {
192	        sessionId, prompt, modeHint: "prototype"
193	   }} to daemon via WS.
194	
195	3. Daemon:
196	     a. picks active skill (prototype-skill)
197	     b. loads design-system (DESIGN.md)
198	     c. materializes a new artifact dir under ./.od/artifacts/<slug>/
199	     d. invokes agent adapter with:
200	          - system: skill's SKILL.md contents + DESIGN.md
201	          - user: original prompt
202	          - cwd: the new artifact dir
203	     e. streams agent events back to web as they arrive:
204	          - "tool_call" (edit file, write file, read file)
205	          - "text_delta"
206	          - "thinking" (if supported)
207	
208	4. Web shows:
209	     - running tool-call feed in the side panel
210	     - artifact tree updates as files materialize
211	     - preview iframe loads the primary output file when agent signals "done"
212	     - slider/comment overlay activates once preview loads
213	
214	5. On completion, daemon appends:
215	     { ts, sessionId, artifactId, action: "generate", skill, promptHash }
216	   to history.jsonl.
217	
218	6. User comments on an element → web sends { method: "session.refine", params: {
219	        sessionId, artifactId, elementId, note }}
220	
221	7. Daemon re-invokes agent with surgical-edit instruction + the note.
222	   Adapter translates based on capabilities:
223	     - Claude Code → native tool loop, edits that region only
224	     - Codex → regenerates the file with "only change element X" constraint
225	     - API fallback → same as Codex path
226	```
227	
228	## 5. Preview renderer
229	
230	**Constraints:**
231	- Must isolate artifact code from the host app (no access to window, cookies, parent DOM).
232	- Must hot-reload as the agent streams writes.
233	- Must support both static HTML and JSX artifacts.
234	
235	**Design:**
236	- Always an `<iframe sandbox="allow-scripts">` — no `allow-same-origin`.
237	- Static HTML: `srcdoc` load of the inlined artifact.
238	- JSX: inject a small bootstrap that imports vendored React 18 + Babel standalone, then dynamically evals the JSX as Babel-transformed code. (This is what [Open CoDesign][ocod] does, and it works; no reason to reinvent.)
239	- Agent writes trigger a debounced rebuild + iframe `srcdoc` replace. Full reload each time — React state loss is acceptable at this scope.
240	
241	## 6. Config files
242	
243	| File | Purpose |
244	|---|---|
245	| `~/.open-design/config.toml` | daemon-global: default agent preference, keys (optional, BYOK), telemetry opt-in (default off) |
246	| `~/.open-design/agents.json` | cached agent detection results |
247	| `./.od/config.json` | project-local: active design system, preferred skills, preferred mode |
248	| `./skills/<skill>/SKILL.md` | skill manifest (standard Claude Code format) |
249	| `./DESIGN.md` | active design system ([awesome-claude-design][acd] format) |
250	
251	All config is plain text / TOML / JSON — no binary formats, no sqlite. Reviewable in PRs.
252	
253	## 7. Protocol between web and daemon
254	
255	The shipped daemon uses HTTP routes plus Server-Sent Events for streaming chat output. This keeps the browser on the same `/api/*` surface in dev and production while still allowing incremental agent output.
256	
257	Representative API surface:
258	
259	```
260	GET  /api/health
261	GET  /api/agents
262	GET  /api/skills
263	GET  /api/design-systems
264	GET  /api/projects
265	POST /api/projects
266	POST /api/import/folder                    # see Folder import
267	GET  /api/projects/:id/files
268	POST /api/projects/:id/upload
269	POST /api/chat              -> text/event-stream
270	POST /api/artifacts/save
271	```
272	
273	### Folder import
274	
275	`POST /api/import/folder` creates a project rooted at an existing local
276	folder instead of the default `.od/projects/<id>/`. The submitted
277	`baseDir` is stored on `metadata.baseDir` and OD reads / writes directly
278	inside it — there is no copy or shadow tree. The user owns the workspace
279	and is responsible for their own version control (git, time machine,
280	etc.), mirroring how Cursor / Claude Code / Aider behave.
281	
282	Safety:
283	
284	- The submitted `baseDir` is canonicalized via `realpath()` before
285	  storage, so user-controlled symlinks cannot redirect later writes.
286	- Standard `resolveSafe` / `sanitizePath` checks apply on every write —
287	  `metadata.baseDir` only changes the project root, not the bounds check.
288	- Imports inside `RUNTIME_DATA_DIR` (the daemon's own data directory) are
289	  refused after symlink resolution.
290	- The file panel hides the conventional build / install dirs
291	  (`node_modules .git dist build .next .nuxt .turbo .cache .output out
292	  coverage __pycache__ .venv vendor target .od .tmp`) so the listing
293	  stays focused on design content.
294	
295	Request / response types: `ImportFolderRequest`, `ImportFolderResponse`
296	in `@open-design/contracts`.
297	
298	#### Desktop folder-import auth (PR #974)
299	
300	The desktop build adds a privileged `shell.openPath` IPC bridge so the

1	# Open Design — Nix flake
2	
3	This flake exposes Open Design as a reproducible package, a `nix run` entry
4	point, a dev shell, and Home Manager / NixOS modules. The architecture
5	mirrors the runtime: the **daemon** (`od` CLI, Express API on `/api/*`)
6	and the **web frontend** (Next.js static SPA at `apps/web/out/`) are
7	**separate packages** and **separate services** — you can run either or
8	both.
9	
10	## Outputs
11	
12	| Output                                     | What it is                                                                             |
13	| ------------------------------------------ | -------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- |
14	| `packages.<system>.daemon`                 | The `@open-design/daemon` package — produces `bin/od`. Default output.                 |
15	| `packages.<system>.web`                    | The Next.js static export (`apps/web/out/`) ready to drop into any static file server. |
16	| `apps.<system>.default`                    | `nix run github:nexu-io/open-design` — boots the daemon.                               |
17	| `devShells.<system>.default`               | Node 24 + Corepack-pinned pnpm 10.33 — reproduces `pnpm install` locally.              |
18	| `homeManagerModules.{default,open-design}` | Home Manager module — primary individual-developer interface.                          |
19	| `nixosModules.{default,open-design}`       | NixOS module — secondary, for shared/server installs.                                  |
20	
21	## Try it without installing
22	
23	```bash
24	nix run github:nexu-io/open-design        # boots the daemon on :7457
25	nix develop github:nexu-io/open-design    # drop into the dev shell
26	```
27	
28	## (1) Home Manager — the recommended path
29	
30	For an individual workstation, add the flake as an input and import the
31	default module:
32	
33	```nix
34	{
35	  inputs.open-design.url = "github:nexu-io/open-design";
36	
37	  outputs = { self, home-manager, open-design, ... }: {
38	    homeConfigurations.you = home-manager.lib.homeManagerConfiguration {
39	      modules = [
40	        open-design.homeManagerModules.default
41	        {
42	          services.open-design = {
43	            enable = true;
44	            autoStart = true;            # systemd --user / launchd agent
45	            webFrontend.enable = true;   # also run the static SPA on :5174
46	          };
47	        }
48	      ];
49	    };
50	  };
51	}
52	```
53	
54	What this wires up:
55	
56	- Linux: `systemd --user` units `open-design.service` and (optionally)
57	  `open-design-web.service`. `systemctl --user status open-design`.
58	- macOS: `launchd` agents `io.nexu.open-design` and (optionally)
59	  `io.nexu.open-design-web`. `launchctl print gui/$UID/io.nexu.open-design`.
60	- Data lives in `$HOME/.od/` by default — override `dataDir` to relocate.
61	
62	## (2) NixOS — for shared/server installs
63	
64	```nix
65	{
66	  imports = [ inputs.open-design.nixosModules.default ];
67	
68	  services.open-design = {
69	    enable = true;
70	    autoStart = true;
71	    openFirewall = true;
72	    webFrontend.enable = true;
73	    user = "open-design";
74	    group = "open-design";
75	  };
76	}
77	```
78	
79	This creates a system user, drops a tmpfiles rule for `/var/lib/open-design`,
80	and runs the daemon under hardened systemd (`ProtectSystem=strict`,
81	`PrivateTmp`, `ReadWritePaths` scoped to the data directory). Use this
82	when you want a single shared instance — for individual user
83	configuration prefer the Home Manager module.
84	
85	## (3) `webFrontend` — when to use it, when to bring your own server
86	
87	Open Design's frontend is a static SPA that issues relative `/api/*`,
88	`/artifacts/*`, and `/frames/*` requests. Three serving options:
89	
90	| Option                                 | When                                                                                                                                                                                                              |
91	| -------------------------------------- | ----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- |
92	| `webFrontend.enable = true`            | You want one-line setup. The module spawns a tiny Caddy file server on `webFrontend.port` (default `5174`) that serves the SPA and reverse-proxies the three path prefixes to the daemon.                         |
93	| `webFrontend.enable = false` (default) | You're running nginx / Caddy / Apache / Traefik yourself. Point your server's document root at `${pkgs.open-design.web}` (or the `packages.<system>.web` output) and replicate the proxy contract in section (4). |
94	| Skip the frontend entirely             | You only need the daemon's API for headless agent dispatch.                                                                                                                                                       |
95	
96	The two services are independent. `autoStart` controls the daemon;
97	`webFrontend.enable` controls the static server. Mix freely.
98	
99	> **Bring-your-own-server gotcha:** if your proxy listens on any
100	> origin that differs from the daemon's bind (different host _or_
101	> different port — even loopback split-port like
102	> `http://127.0.0.1:8080` while the daemon stays on `:7457`), the
103	> daemon's same-origin gate will 403 the SPA's writes until you tell
104	> it about that origin. Either set
105	> `services.open-design.webFrontend.allowedOrigins = [ "<your-proxy-origin>" ]`
106	> (which feeds `OD_ALLOWED_ORIGINS`) or, for the loopback-only
107	> split-port case, set `extraEnv.OD_WEB_PORT = "<proxy-port>"`. See
108	> section (4) for the full decision tree.
109	
110	### Exposing the bundled frontend on a non-loopback host
111	
112	By default `webFrontend.host = "127.0.0.1"` so enabling the bundled
113	caddy does not publish anything beyond loopback. To intentionally
114	share with a LAN, two settings must be widened together — the
115	modules assert at eval time that the second is set whenever the
116	first is widened:
117	
118	```nix
119	services.open-design.webFrontend = {
120	  enable = true;
121	  host = "0.0.0.0";  # caddy listener
122	  # Every external origin browsers will load the SPA from. The daemon
123	  # matches each entry against the browser's `Origin` header AND adds
124	  # its host:port to the `Host`-header allowlist (Caddy v2 reverse_proxy
125	  # preserves the original Host upstream by default), so list each
126	  # scheme + hostname combo you actually use.
127	  allowedOrigins = [
128	    "http://laptop.local:5174"
129	    "https://laptop.local:5174"
130	  ];
131	};
132	# On NixOS you also need:
133	services.open-design.openFirewall = true;
134	```
135	
136	Under the hood `allowedOrigins` is forwarded to the daemon as the
137	`OD_ALLOWED_ORIGINS` environment variable (comma-separated). If you
138	run the daemon outside the modules — for example, behind your own
139	nginx/caddy — set `OD_ALLOWED_ORIGINS` directly in the daemon's
140	environment with the same shape:
141	
142	```
143	OD_ALLOWED_ORIGINS=http://host1:port,https://host1:port,http://host2:port
144	```
145	
146	Each entry must be a bare origin (`scheme://host[:port]`); only
147	`http://` and `https://` schemes are accepted, and the daemon refuses
148	to start if any entry fails to parse. The variable widens only the
149	general `/api/*` same-origin gate — connector-credential and
150	live-artifact preview/refresh routes stay strictly loopback-only by

content.content:
---
name: blog-post
description: |
  A long-form article / blog post — masthead, hero image placeholder,
  article body with figures and pull quotes, author byline, related posts.
  Use when the brief asks for "blog", "article", "post", "essay", or
  "case study".
triggers:
  - "blog"
  - "blog post"
  - "article"
  - "essay"
  - "case study"
  - "newsletter"
  - "博客"
  - "文章"
od:
  mode: prototype
  platform: desktop
  scenario: marketing
  preview:
    type: html
    entry: index.html
  design_system:
    requires: true
    sections: [color, typography, layout, components]
  craft:
    requires: [typography, typography-hierarchy, typography-hierarchy-editorial, rtl-and-bidi]
---

# Blog Post Skill

Produce a single long-form article page — editorial layout, no chrome.

## Workflow

1. **Read the active DESIGN.md** (injected above). Lean into the typography
   tokens — long-form is 70% type, 20% image, 10% chrome.
2. **Pick the topic** from the brief and write a real article — at least 600
   words across 4–6 H2 sections. No lorem ipsum.
3. **Sections**, in order:
   - **Masthead** — small wordmark + 4–6 nav links, plain.
   - **Article header** — category eyebrow, headline (display token, large),
     deck (1–2 sentence subhead), author name + role + date.
   - **Hero image** — a 16:9 placeholder block using a DS-tinted gradient or
     solid fill (no external images). Add a 1-line caption underneath.
   - **Body** — alternating prose paragraphs with at least:
     - 1 pull quote (large display type, accent rule on the inline-start edge so the layout flips correctly under `dir="rtl"`).
     - 1 figure (image placeholder + caption).
     - 1 list (numbered or bulleted).

1	# Skills Protocol
2	
3	**Parent:** [`spec.md`](spec.md) · **Siblings:** [`skills-contributing.md`](skills-contributing.md) · [`architecture.md`](architecture.md) · [`agent-adapters.md`](agent-adapters.md) · [`modes.md`](modes.md)
4	
5	> Want to ship a skill upstream rather than read the protocol spec? See [`skills-contributing.md`](skills-contributing.md) — quick start, merge bar, PR template, common rejections. This file is the **what** (frontmatter grammar, discovery rules, mode semantics); that file is the **how** (clone to merged PR).
6	
7	A **Skill** is the atomic unit of design capability in OD. We adopt Claude Code's `SKILL.md` convention verbatim as the base format, then add optional fields for design-specific features (preview type, input schema, slider parameters). A skill written for plain Claude Code runs in OD. An OD skill that doesn't use our extensions runs in plain Claude Code.
8	
9	> **Compatibility promise:** A skill like [`guizang-ppt-skill`](https://github.com/op7418/guizang-ppt-skill) works in OD **without modification**. It just drops into `~/.claude/skills/` and OD discovers it.
10	
11	---
12	
13	## 1. Base format (unchanged from Claude Code)
14	
15	Every skill is a directory containing at minimum a `SKILL.md`:
16	
17	```
18	<skill-root>/
19	├── SKILL.md              # manifest + workflow instructions
20	├── assets/               # templates, images, boilerplate the skill writes
21	│   └── …
22	└── references/           # knowledge files the skill reads during planning
23	    ├── components.md
24	    ├── layouts.md
25	    └── …
26	```
27	
28	`SKILL.md` front-matter (YAML):
29	
30	```yaml
31	---
32	name: magazine-web-ppt
33	zh_name: "杂志风网页 PPT"
34	en_name: "Magazine Web PPT"
35	description: |
36	  Magazine-style horizontal-swipe web deck.
37	  Trigger keywords: 杂志风 PPT, magazine deck, swipe slides.
38	zh_description: "杂志风横向翻页网页 PPT。"
39	en_description: |
40	  Magazine-style horizontal-swipe web deck.
41	  Trigger keywords: 杂志风 PPT, magazine deck, swipe slides.
42	triggers:
43	  - "magazine deck"
44	  - "杂志风 PPT"
45	  - "horizontal swipe presentation"
46	---
47	```
48	
49	Body is free-form Markdown that describes the workflow the agent should follow — typically a numbered step list plus principles. This is what [guizang-ppt-skill](https://github.com/op7418/guizang-ppt-skill) does.
50	
51	**OD reads all of this as-is.** No changes required.
52	
53	## 2. OD extensions (optional)
54	
55	Skills can declare additional front-matter fields to unlock OD-specific UI. All fields are optional; absent fields fall back to sensible defaults.
56	
57	```yaml
58	---
59	name: magazine-web-ppt
60	description: …
61	triggers: […]
62	
63	# --- OD extensions below this line ---
64	
65	od:
66	  mode: deck                        # one of: prototype | deck | template | design-system
67	  preview:
68	    type: html                      # html | jsx | pptx | markdown
69	    entry: index.html               # relative path produced by the skill
70	    reload: debounce-100            # how the preview refreshes
71	  example_prompt: "Create a magazine-style web deck from my content."
72	  example_prompt_i18n:
73	    zh-CN: "用杂志风网页 PPT 模板把我的内容做成横向翻页 deck。"
74	  design_system:
75	    requires: true                  # this skill reads the active DESIGN.md
76	    sections: [color, typography]   # which sections it actually uses (for prompt pruning)
77	  craft:                            # universal, brand-agnostic craft references
78	    requires: [typography, color, anti-ai-slop]
79	  inputs:                           # typed inputs the user can fill in the UI
80	    - name: title
81	      type: string
82	      required: true
83	    - name: slide_count
84	      type: integer
85	      default: 8
86	      min: 4
87	      max: 20
88	    - name: theme
89	      type: enum
90	      values: [editorial, minimal, brutalist, dark-glass, warm]
91	      default: editorial
92	  parameters:                       # live-tweakable sliders after first generation
93	    - name: accent_hue
94	      type: hue                     # hue | spacing | font-scale | opacity
95	      default: 18
96	      range: [0, 360]
97	    - name: section_spacing
98	      type: spacing
99	      default: 48
100	      range: [16, 128]
101	  outputs:
102	    primary: index.html
103	    secondary: [slides.json]        # for PPTX export
104	  capabilities_required:
105	    - surgical_edit                 # comment mode needs this
106	    - file_write
107	---
108	```
109	
110	### 2.1 What OD uses each field for
111	
112	| Field | Used by |
113	|---|---|
114	| `zh_name` / `en_name` | localized picker title; falls back to `name` |
115	| `zh_description` / `en_description` | localized picker description; falls back to `description` |
116	| `od.mode` | routing (which mode picker the skill shows up under) |
117	| `od.preview.type` | picking the right iframe renderer |
118	| `od.example_prompt` | English fallback starter prompt used by picker CTA |
119	| `od.example_prompt_i18n` | localized starter prompt map (for example `zh-CN`) |
120	| `od.design_system.requires` | whether to inject `DESIGN.md` |
121	| `od.design_system.sections` | pruning the injected DESIGN.md to relevant sections only (token savings) |
122	| `od.craft.requires` | which brand-agnostic `craft/<slug>.md` references to inject (e.g. `typography`, `color`, `anti-ai-slop`); injected between DESIGN.md and the skill body |
123	| `od.inputs` | rendering a typed form in the sidebar instead of only free-text |
124	| `od.parameters` | rendering live sliders that re-prompt on change |
125	| `od.outputs.primary` | which file the iframe loads |
126	| `od.outputs.secondary` | which files export pipelines read (e.g. `slides.json` for PPTX) |
127	| `od.capabilities_required` | gating: if the active agent lacks surgical edit, comment mode is disabled for this skill |
128	
129	### 2.2 If a skill omits `od:` entirely
130	
131	Defaults:
132	- `mode`: inferred from name/description (best-effort keyword match) or "prototype"
133	- `preview.type`: sniff for `*.html` → html, `*.jsx` → jsx, else "markdown"
134	- `preview.entry`: first file matching the sniffed type
135	- `design_system.requires`: true if the skill body mentions "design system" or "DESIGN.md"
136	- `inputs`, `parameters`: none (free-text prompt only)
137	
138	The goal: **zero-config compatibility** for existing Claude Code skills.
139	
140	## 3. Skill discovery & precedence
141	
142	The daemon's skill registry scans three locations:
143	
144	| Location | Priority | Purpose |
145	|---|---|---|
146	| `./.claude/skills/` | 1 (highest) | project-private skills, not committed |
147	| `./skills/` | 2 | project-committed skills |
148	| `~/.claude/skills/` | 3 | user-global skills |
149	
150	Conflicts by `name` resolve to the higher-priority version. All locations are watched with `chokidar` in dev and re-scanned on `SIGHUP` in production.
151	
152	### Symlink strategy (borrowed from [cc-switch](https://github.com/farion1231/cc-switch))
153	
154	`cc-switch` maintains a central skill dir at `~/.cc-switch/skills/` and symlinks it into each agent's expected location (`~/.claude/skills/`, `~/.codex/skills/`, etc.). OD can opt into the same model:
155	
156	```
157	~/.open-design/skills/
158	    magazine-web-ppt/      (canonical location)
159	~/.claude/skills/
160	    magazine-web-ppt → ~/.open-design/skills/magazine-web-ppt
161	~/.codex/skills/
162	    magazine-web-ppt → ~/.open-design/skills/magazine-web-ppt
163	```
164	
165	One install → every agent sees the skill. This is optional; users who only use one agent don't need it.
166	
167	## 4. Skill types (by mode)
168	
169	Each mode expects a slightly different skill shape. The required outputs and expected workflow differ.
170	
171	### 4.1 `prototype-skill`
172	
173	- **Purpose:** single-screen interactive prototype.
174	- **Preview:** `html` or `jsx`.
175	- **Primary output:** `index.html` or `Prototype.jsx`.
176	- **Typical workflow:** clarify brief → resolve design tokens → write component tree → write file.
177	- **Example skills:** `saas-landing`, `dashboard`, `login-flow`, `empty-states`.
178	
179	### 4.2 `deck-skill`
180	
181	- **Purpose:** multi-slide presentation.
182	- **Preview:** `html` (single-file deck with in-page navigation).
183	- **Primary output:** `index.html`.
184	- **Secondary output:** `slides.json` (for PPTX export).
185	- **Typical workflow:** clarify topic + slide count → pick theme → populate slides from layout catalog → self-check against quality rubric.
186	- **Reference implementation:** [guizang-ppt-skill](https://github.com/op7418/guizang-ppt-skill) — fork this for v1.
187	
188	### 4.3 `template-skill`
189	
190	- **Purpose:** start from a pre-built artifact; agent only personalizes content, doesn't design from scratch.
191	- **Preview:** inherits from the template bundle (`html` typically).
192	- **Primary output:** a populated copy of the template.
193	- **Typical workflow:** copy `assets/template/` to artifact dir → replace content placeholders → optionally tweak tokens to match design system.
194	- **Why separate from `prototype-skill`:** much faster (no design decisions), higher-quality floor, worse ceiling.
195	
196	### 4.4 `design-system-skill`
197	
198	- **Purpose:** produce a `DESIGN.md` from inputs (brand brief, screenshot, URL).
199	- **Preview:** `markdown` (render the resulting DESIGN.md with a sample-components preview).
200	- **Primary output:** `DESIGN.md`.

content.text:
Excellent! Now I have comprehensive coverage. Let me compile the final report:

## Comprehensive Overview of Open Design

**Open Design** is an **open-source, local-first alternative to Claude Design** (Anthropic's paid design tool). It's a sophisticated design generation and artifact system that integrates with coding agents to enable AI-assisted design workflows. Released in April 2026, it has gained significant community traction (40k+ GitHub stars in two weeks) and is now at version 0.8.0.

---

## **What This Project Is**

Open Design is a **modular design workspace** where users can:
- Generate design artifacts (landing pages, dashboards, mobile prototypes, decks, etc.) using AI agents
- Access 150+ design systems (Linear, Stripe, Vercel, Apple, Notion, etc.)
- Use 131+ composable skills (functional templates for different design scenarios)
- Work with auto-detected coding agents (Claude Code, Codex, Cursor, Gemini, Copilot, Qwen, DeepSeek, etc.)
- Iterate on designs through interactive forms, live previews, and chatbot-driven refinement
- Export in multiple formats (HTML, PDF, PPTX, ZIP, Markdown)

**Core philosophy:** Don't ship an agent—use the ones already on the user's machine. Wire them into a skill-driven design workflow with deterministic design systems and an anti-AI-slop quality gate.

---

## **Main Apps & Packages**

### **Apps** (in `/apps/`)

| App | Purpose | Stack |
|---|---|---|
| **daemon** | Central Node.js/Express server; owns `/api/*`, agent spawning, skills, design systems, artifacts, SQLite projects DB | Node 24, Express, better-sqlite3, MCP SDK |
| **web** | Next.js 16 App Router frontend; chat, file workspace, iframe preview, settings | Next.js 16, React 18, Tailwind, TypeScript |
| **desktop** | Optional Electron shell; discovers web URL through sidecar IPC | Electron 41, IPC-based coordination |
| **packaged** | Thin Electron entry point for desktop releases; owns `od://` URI handling | Electron packaging layer |
| **landing-page** | Marketing website (open-design.ai) | Next.js |
| **telemetry-worker** | Optional analytics pipeline | Cloudflare Workers |

### **Packages** (in `/packages/`)

| Package | Purpose |
|---|---|
| **contracts** | Shared TypeScript web/daemon API contracts (DTOs, SSE shapes, error types) |
| **sidecar-proto** | Open Design sidecar business protocol definition |
| **sidecar** | Generic sidecar runtime primitives (process spawn, IPC, lifecycle) |
| **platform** | Generic OS/process abstractions (Windows/macOS/Linux) |
| **host** | Daemon/web app host integration layer |
| **agui-adapter** | AGUI (Agent Client Protocol) adapter for multi-agent CLI support |
| **registry-protocol** | Plugin registry protocol |
| **plugin-runtime** | Plugin execution runtime |
| **diagnostics** | Health checks and troubleshooting |
| **download** | Download utilities (for releases) |

### **Tools** (in `/tools/`)

| Tool | Purpose |
|---|---|
| **tools-dev** | Local development lifecycle: start/stop/logs/status for daemon + web + desktop |
| **tools-pack** | Packaged build/updater harness; Mac beta release, installer identity, version management |
| **tools-pr** | Maintainer PR-duty tool: review lane classification, forbidden surfaces, checklists |
| **tools-serve** | Fixture service: local test updater metadata server |

---

## **Key Technologies & Architecture**

### **Tech Stack**
- **Runtime:** Node.js ~24, pnpm 10.33.2, Corepack
- **Frontend:** Next.js 16 (App Router), React 18, Tailwind CSS 4, TypeScript
- **Backend:** Express, better-sqlite3 (WAL mode), Server-Sent Events (SSE)
- **Desktop:** Electron 41, IPC over Unix sockets
- **Storage:** SQLite at `.od/app.sqlite`; plain files at `.od/projects/<id>/`
- **Build:** esbuild, TypeScript compiler, Vitest (unit), Playwright (E2E)
- **Deployment:** Docker (Alpine), Vercel web layer, Nix flake, GitHub Actions

### **System Architecture**

```
Browser (Next.js SPA)
    ↓ http://localhost:7456 (or tunnel)
Local Daemon (Express + SQLite)
    ├─ Agent adapter pool (detects Claude Code, Codex, Cursor, Gemini, etc.)
    ├─ Skills registry (scans ~/.claude/skills/, ./skills/, ./.claude/skills/)
    ├─ Design system resolver (reads DESIGN.md files)
    └─ Artifact store (files in .od/projects/<id>/)
```

**Three deployment topologies:**
1. **Fully local** (default): Browser → daemon → agents on same machine
2. **Web on Vercel + daemon local:** Vercel frontend connects to user's laptop daemon via tunnel
3. **Web on Vercel only:** No daemon, degraded experience (no CLI, no file artifacts)

### **Sidecar IPC Pattern**
- Desktop app discovers web URL via `tools-dev` sidecar IPC
- Named pipes on Windows, Unix sockets on macOS/Linux
- Namespaced at `~/.tmp/tools-dev/<namespace>/` with `<app>.sock` sockets

---

## **Capabilities & Feature Set**

### **Design Generation**
- **Interactive question form** (turn-1): Surface, audience, tone, brand context, scale, constraints
- **Visual direction picker** (turn-2, optional): 5 curated schools (Editorial, Modern Minimal, Tech Utility, Brutalist, Soft Warm) with deterministic OKLch palettes + font stacks
- **TodoWrite streaming:** Live task-list progress as the agent works
- **Five-dimensional critique:** Philosophy, Hierarchy, Detail, Function, Innovation
- **Live preview iframe:** Sandboxed (`srcdoc`), hot-reloads as agent writes

### **131+ Skills** (in `/design-templates/` and `/skills/`)
- **Prototypes (32):** web-prototype, saas-landing, dashboard, pricing-page, docs-page, blog-post, mobile-app, mobile-onboarding, gamified-app, email-marketing, social-carousel, magazine-poster, motion-frames, sprite-animation, dating-web, digital-eguide, wireframe-sketch, critique, tweaks, etc.
- **Decks (9):** guizang-ppt (default), simple-deck, replit-deck, weekly-update, plus HTML PPT variants
- **Office/Operations:** pm-spec, team-okrs, meeting-notes, kanban-board, eng-runbook, finance-report, invoice, hr-onboarding
- **Media generation:** image (gpt-image-2), video (Seedance 2.0, HyperFrames), audio (ElevenLabs)
- **Utilities:** design-md, brand-guidelines, color-expert, creative-director, data-report, etc.

### **150+ Design Systems** (in `/design-systems/`)
- **Hand-authored starters:** `default` (Neutral Modern), `warm-editorial`
- **Product systems:** Linear, Stripe, Vercel, Airbnb, Tesla, Notion, Apple, Anthropic, Cursor, Supabase, Figma, etc.
- **Design skills:** 57 systems from awesome-design-skills
- **Each system:** 9-section `DESIGN.md` (color, typography, spacing, layout, components, motion, voice, brand, anti-patterns)

### **Craft References** (in `/craft/`)
Brand-agnostic design rules injected into prompts:
- typography, typography-hierarchy, typography-hierarchy-editorial
- color, animation-discipline, accessibility-baseline, rtl-and-bidi
- form-validation, state-coverage, laws-of-ux, anti-ai-slop

### **Agent Support**
**16 auto-detected coding CLIs:**
- Claude Code, Codex, Devin, Cursor Agent, Gemini, OpenCode, Qwen, Qoder, GitHub Copilot, Hermes, Kimi, Pi, Kiro, Kilo, Mistral Vibe, DeepSeek
- **BYOK fallback:** OpenAI-compatible proxy at `/api/proxy/{anthropic,openai,azure,google,ollama,senseaudio}/stream`

### **Artifacts & Export**
- Sandboxed iframe preview (React 18 + Babel standalone for JSX)
- **Export formats:** HTML (self-contained), PDF (browser print), PPTX (agent-driven), ZIP, Markdown
- **Persistence:** SQLite + plain files (git-friendly)
- **Live artifacts:** Refreshable designs with source data snapshot

### **Media Generation Surfaces**
- **Images:** gpt-image-2 for posters, avatars, infographics (93 prompt templates)
- **Video:** Seedance 2.0 for 15s text-to-video; HyperFrames for HTML→MP4 motion graphics
- **Audio:** ElevenLabs speech + sound effects
- All outputs saved to project workspace as `.mp4` / `.png` chips

### **MCP Server**
- Stdio MCP exposes Open Design projects to Claude Code, Cursor, VS Code, etc.
- Read-only: `search_files`, `get_file`, `get_artifact`
- Defaults to active project in Open Design when no project specified

---

## **Directory Structure**

```
open-design/
├── README.md (English + 11 translations)
├── CONTRIBUTING.md (+ translations)
├── QUICKSTART.md (+ translations)
├── AGENTS.md                    ← Single source of truth for developers
│
├── apps/
│   ├── daemon/                  ← Node.js/Express server + `od` CLI
│   ├── web/                     ← Next.js 16 frontend
│   ├── desktop/                 ← Electron shell
│   ├── packaged/                ← Electron entry for releases
│   └── landing-page/            ← Marketing site
│
├── packages/
│   ├── contracts/               ← Shared API DTOs
│   ├── sidecar-proto/           ← Sidecar protocol
│   ├── sidecar/                 ← Generic sidecar runtime
│   ├── platform/                ← OS abstractions
│   ├── host/                    ← Integration layer
│   └── [others]
│
├── tools/
│   ├── dev/                     ← Lifecycle control (pnpm tools-dev)
│   ├── pack/                    ← Build/updater harness
│   ├── pr/                      ← PR-duty tool
│   └── serve/                   ← Fixture service
│
├── design-templates/            ← 131+ skills (prototype, deck, image, video, audio, utility)
├── design-systems/              ← 150+ DESIGN.md systems
├── craft/                       ← 10 brand-agnostic rule files
├── skills/                      ← Alias to design-templates (legacy)
├── assets/frames/               ← Device frames (iPhone, Pixel, iPad, MacBook, Chrome)
│
├── docs/                        ← Architecture, protocols, guidelines
│   ├── spec.md                  ← Product specification
│   ├── architecture.md          ← System topology & data flow
│   ├── skills-protocol.md       ← SKILL.md format & discovery
│   ├── agent-adapters.md        ← Per-CLI support matrix
│   ├── modes.md                 ← prototype/deck/template/design-system modes
│   └── [others]
│
├── deploy/                      ← Docker Compose, scripts
├── nix/                         ← Nix flake (Home Manager, NixOS)
├── plugins/                     ← Plugin system (spec, registry, community)
├── .od/                         ← Runtime data (gitignored, auto-created)
├── .github/                     ← GH Actions workflows
└── flake.nix, pnpm-lock.yaml, etc.
```

---

## **Key Configuration Files**

| File | Purpose |
|---|---|
| `package.json` | Root workspace; exports `od` bin; pnpm overrides |
| `pnpm-workspace.yaml` | Workspace roots: `apps/*`, `packages/*`, `tools/*`, `e2e` |
| `.node-version` | Node.js version (pinned to 24) |
| `.github/workflows/` | CI/CD: tests, Docker builds, releases, updater publish |
| `AGENTS.md` | Onboarding for new developers; architecture, conventions, boundary rules |
| `CONTRIBUTING.md` | Contribution guidelines + localized versions |
| `CONTEXT.md` | Domain language glossary (Project, Artifact, Skill, etc.) |
| `flake.nix` | Nix reproducible build |

---

## **Development Workflow**

**Single entry point:** `pnpm tools-dev`

```bash
pnpm install                      # Setup (Node 24, pnpm 10.33.2)
pnpm tools-dev run web            # Start daemon + web locally
pnpm tools-dev                    # Start daemon + web + desktop
pnpm tools-dev status             # Check running processes
pnpm tools-dev logs               # View daemon/web/desktop tails
pnpm tools-dev stop               # Stop all
pnpm guard                        # Linter + policy checks
pnpm typecheck                    # Type validation
```

**Validation gates:**
- Pre-commit (style policy, i18n coverage)
- GitHub Actions (typecheck, tests, builds)
- Local socket IPC namespace isolation (prevents daemon port conflicts)

---

## **Release Channels & Distribution**

- **stable:** Production releases (macOS DMG, Windows installer, Linux AppImage)
- **beta:** Daily R&D validation
- **nightly:** Internal validation gate for stable
- **preview:** Early access with preview version tags (`X.Y.Z-preview.N`)
- **Desktop app:** [open-design.ai](https://open-design.ai), GitHub releases
- **Docker:** `vanjayak/open-design` on Docker Hub
- **Nix:** `github:nexu-io/open-design` flake

---

## **Community & Extensibility**

- **Skills are files**, not plugins — add a folder to `/skills/` or `~/.claude/skills/`, restart daemon, it's in the picker
- **Design systems are Markdown** — fork a `DESIGN.md`, no theme JSON
- **Craft references are versioned docs** — extensible, forward-compatible
- **Plugins system** (in `/plugins/`) — spec, registry, community gallery
- **Translations:** 18 locales (en, de, es, fr, ja, pt-BR, ru, zh-CN, zh-TW, ar, fa, hu, id, ko, pl, th, tr, uk)

---

## **What Makes It Unique**

1. **No lock-in:** Works with any coding agent on your `PATH`; BYOK at every layer
2. **Deterministic design:** 5 visual directions with fixed OKLch palettes + font stacks—no AI freestyle
3. **Interactive question form:** Locks brief constraints before generation (60% of redirects prevented)
4. **Local-first:** All artifacts on disk; projects in SQLite; no cloud dependency for design data
5. **Vercel-deployable web layer:** Same SPA runs locally or on Vercel + remote daemon
6. **Skill-driven system:** 131+ reusable design templates, each with optional design-system injection and craft references
7. **Open foundations:** Built on 4 open-source shoulders (huashu-design, guizang-ppt-skill, open-codesign, multica)

---

This is a **production-grade design system** with **enterprise-scale architecture**, **multi-agent support**, and a **thriving contributor ecosystem**—all under Apache-2.0 open source.
